tier2.quote: Our one year Interventional Cardiology program is fully accredited by the ACGME. With a referral base of over 2 million individuals in Fresno and the Central California region, we have one of the most diverse populations in the country.
tier2.claimText: Interventional Cardiology Fellowship — one-year ACGME-accredited.
tier2.whyA1Missed: A1 enumerated only a subset of the fellowship list; Interventional Cardiology paragraph was skipped.
tier2.tierAssignmentRationale: ACGME-accredited subspecialty fellowship for trained physicians — Tier 2.

tier2.quote: The UCSF Fresno Hematology Oncology Fellowship Program was founded in 2016. Graduates of our program attain the professional, attitudinal, cognitive, and technical skills necessary to provide their patients with kind and compassionate care.
tier2.claimText: Hematology/Oncology Fellowship offered at UCSF Fresno (founded 2016).
tier2.whyA1Missed: A1 stopped after a partial fellowship pass; Heme/Onc paragraph was not captured.

tier2.quote: The Infectious Diseases Fellowship Program at UCSF Fresno offers comprehensive clinical training and research opportunities in one of the largest medical centers in Central California. Two highly qualified physicians are offered positions in our program annually.
tier2.claimText: Infectious Diseases Fellowship — accepts 2 physicians/year at UCSF Fresno.
tier2.whyA1Missed: A1 did not enumerate ID fellowship paragraph.
tier2.tierAssignmentRationale: Subspecialty fellowship for trained physicians — Tier 2.

tier2.quote: Our program provides comprehensive training in gastroenterology, hepatology, and endoscopic procedures. Our fellows are exposed to an exceptionally diverse patient population with a wide spectrum of gastrointestinal and liver diseases.
tier2.claimText: Gastroenterology Fellowship offered at UCSF Fresno.
tier2.whyA1Missed: GI fellowship paragraph not enumerated by A1.

tier2.quote: Our Cardiovascular Fellowship provides extensive clinical, academic and research experiences in state-of-the-art-settings with a diverse patient population. Fellows gain exposure to a wide variety of pathologies and rare cardiovascular conditions not seen in many major centers on a routine basis.
tier2.claimText: Cardiovascular Disease Fellowship offered at UCSF Fresno.
tier2.whyA1Missed: Cardiovascular Disease fellowship paragraph not captured by A1.

tier2.quote: The one-year Hospice and Palliative Medicine (HPM) fellowship is an integral part of the fully accredited Family Medicine Residency Program.
tier2.claimText: Hospice and Palliative Medicine Fellowship — one-year, within Family Medicine Residency.
tier2.whyA1Missed: HPM fellowship paragraph not enumerated.
tier2.tierAssignmentRationale: Subspecialty fellowship for trained physicians — Tier 2.

tier2.quote: We are excited to introduce our new Hospital Medicine Fellowship Program that will expand the career paths for the future leaders in Hospital Medicine offering training in research, teaching, and clinical experience with supervision and mentorship of very experienced academic hospital medicine faculty.
tier2.claimText: Hospital Medicine Fellowship offered at UCSF Fresno.
tier2.whyA1Missed: Hospital Medicine fellowship paragraph not enumerated.

tier2.quote: The one-year Maternal Child Health Fellowship Program (MCH) is a collaboration between the Family Medicine and Obstetrics/Gynecology Residency programs at UCSF Fresno.
tier2.claimText: Maternal Child Health Fellowship — one-year, joint Family Medicine/OB-GYN.
tier2.whyA1Missed: MCH fellowship paragraph not enumerated.

tier2.quote: This 0.6 FTE clinical duties schedule affords the fellow plenty of time to participate in required curricular activities that develop the cognitive knowledge and skill sets expected of a Wilderness Medicine Fellow
tier2.claimText: Wilderness Medicine Fellowship offered at UCSF Fresno (0.6 FTE clinical schedule).
tier2.whyA1Missed: Wilderness Medicine fellowship paragraph not enumerated.

tier2.quote: The inpatient/outpatient Hepatology Fellowship Program introduces the fellow to inpatient/outpatient management of patients with the wide range of acute and chronic liver diseases.
tier2.claimText: Hepatology Fellowship offered at UCSF Fresno.
tier2.whyA1Missed: Hepatology fellowship paragraph not enumerated.

tier2.quote: Our Emergency Medicine residency program was founded in 1974 and is one of the oldest and most respected in the country.
tier2.claimText: Emergency Medicine Education Fellowship; EM Residency founded 1974 at UCSF Fresno.
tier2.whyA1Missed: EM Education Fellowship and EM Ultrasound Fellowship paragraphs were not separately captured.

tier2.quote: The UCSF Fresno Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery Program is a four-year program, fully accredited and approved by the American Dental Association, and currently have a house staff of 16 residents (four residents per year).
tier2.claimText: Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery Residency — four-year ADA-accredited, 16 residents (4/year).
tier2.whyA1Missed: A1's residency claim was the nav list only; detailed OMFS paragraph with duration/cohort size was not captured separately.
tier2.tierAssignmentRationale: Residency program duration and class size — Tier 2.

tier2.quote: The UCSF Fresno Family Medicine Residency is a dynamic program which has graduated more than 400 residents since its inception in 1970.
tier2.claimText: Family and Community Medicine Residency at UCSF Fresno — 400+ graduates since 1970.
tier2.whyA1Missed: A1 captured only the nav list of residencies; the descriptive Family Medicine paragraph with program history was not separately emitted.

tier3.quote: The goal of this program is to provide dentists, technicians, hygienists, students and other dental professionals the opportunity to participate in locally based continuing education seminars needed to maintain professional licenses.

tier3.whyA1Missed: A1 captured CME (f11) but not the parallel Continuing Dental Education program for practicing dental professionals.
